title: Ep 77 Why Is My Dog Barking at the TV? (And What You Can Do About It) & Too Close for Comfort
podcast_name: Help! My Dog: The Podcast. Dog Behaviour & Training Strategies that Work!
summary: Have you ever felt like your dog has a personal vendetta against the TV? You’re not alone! In this episode, we’re diving into a listener's question about a dog who just can’t stop barking at animals on the screen. It’s a surprisingly common problem with surprisingly few answers online, and it often leaves owners feeling stuck and overwhelmed (possibly with broken TVs!). Why is your dog reacting like that to the latest Attenborough documentary as if elephants and giraffes just invaded your living room? And more importantly, what can you actually do about it? Tune in as we unpack the hidden emotions behind TV reactivity and how you can begin to shift your dog’s response. ................ Whether your dog is unpredictable with other dogs or predictably bad… Whether it looks like freezing, barking, lunging or running away… Whether you are just starting out on your dog-owning journey or your dog has been labelled as having a “behaviour problem”… … this episode is for you and is going to give you huge insights! Want to join the Help! My Dog Online HUB? Here's the link: www.helpmydog.com/hub Dr Tom Mitchell is a world-renowned veterinarian, behaviourist and dog trainer and director of the Behavet behaviour clinic (rated Excellent on Trustpilot), which sees over 400 pet behaviour consultations each and every month no matter where you are in the world. In 2023, he founded Help! My Dog, an online dog behaviour learning platform to help even more owners all over the world and show that dog behaviour transformation is not only possible but it's FUN, with the right know-how! Make sure to subscribe/follow the podcast for the free weekly episodes! Useful Links: Follow Help! title: "LEE ASHER of THE ASHER HOUSE"
podcast_name: Fur Real with Mark A Kyle
summary: Lee Asher......You’ve seen him on social media rescuing animals, transforming lives, and living his dream surrounded by a pack of pups — now hear the story behind the mission. Lee opens up about what inspired him to leave his corporate life behind, how he built a sanctuary (The Asher House) from scratch, and what keeps him going through the chaos (and joy!) of caring for dozens of dogs.  From road trips in an RV with his rescue crew to the creation of a permanent haven for animals in need, Lee shares the highs, the heartbreaks, and the moments that changed him. He also dives into the deeper “why” behind his work, the challenges of staying authentic in the public eye, and what it really means to build a life with purpose. This episode is full of heart, humor, and the kind of real talk that’ll inspire you to chase your passion — even if it’s covered in fur.  www.theasherhouse.com              www.furrealpodcast.com  ig@theasherhouse          ig@leeasherworld    ig@thefurrealpodcast       ig@markakyle  fb@ The Asher House      fb@ Mark A Kyle   tik tok @theasherhouse    @thefurrealpodcast  Speical thanks to J Jig Cicero @jjigcicero for our music intro and outro..you rock!!! title: Breaking Barriers: Understanding Virtus and the Future of Para Equestrian Sports: With Danielle Curzon
podcast_name: Let's Talk Horse Business
summary: In this inspiring episode of the Let's Talk Horse Business podcast, George interviews Danielle Curzon, a remarkable para rider who has overcome significant health challenges to return to the equestrian world. Danielle shares her journey from a successful childhood in horse showing to a diverse career in security and martial arts, before facing health issues that led to a stroke and chronic conditions. Despite these obstacles, she found solace and purpose in horses, particularly through her partnership with her stallion, Pleasureland AM, and her mare, Dizzy Rascal. Danielle discusses her experiences with Virtus Sports, the international governing body for intellectually impaired athletes, and her aspirations to compete at the World Championships. She emphasizes the importance of support systems, natural health solutions, and the therapeutic benefits of horses for mental health, offering valuable insights for anyone facing challenges in their own lives.  KEY TAKEAWAYS    Resilience Through Adversity: The journey highlights the importance of resilience, showcasing how challenges such as health issues and career changes can lead to new opportunities and personal growth.    Support Systems Matter: A strong support network, including sponsors, trainers, and family, plays a crucial role in overcoming obstacles and achieving goals in the equestrian world.    Mental Health Awareness: The discussion emphasizes the significance of mental health, particularly in the equestrian community, and the therapeutic benefits that horses can provide for individuals facing mental health challenges.    Natural Healing Approaches: There is a focus on exploring natural pathways for health and wellness, including the use of alternative therapies and nutrition, which can be beneficial for both riders and their horses.    Pursuing Passion and Purpose: The narrative encourages individuals to follow their passions, adapt to changes, and seek fulfillment in their pursuits, whether in equestrian sports or other areas of life.    Socials  https://www.instagram.com/gbetson/  www.tiktok.com/@georgebetson  About the Host  George Betson is a seasoned business entrepreneur with over a decade of experience, driven by a passion for both strategic business growth and the equine world. Starting his journey as a business graduate, George worked alongside his late father to run the family business, learning firsthand the value of hard work, resilience, and the essentials of building a successful enterprise.     This Podcast has been brought to you by Disruptive Media. https://disruptivemedia.co.uk/

title: Ep. 10. Dog Growling at Baby? Understanding Toddler Behavior, Body Language & Preventing Bites
podcast_name: Dog and Baby Podcast: Dog training tips for expecting parents
summary: If your dog has been acting different since your baby started crawling or if you're constantly on edge wondering if things are about to go sideways this episode is for you. We’re talking I'm answering a listener question that I believe many parents can relate to. From growling at the food bowl to pacing around the baby, I’m breaking down exactly why this happens, what your dog is trying to communicate, and how toddler development plays a major role in dog behavior. This is not just about training your dog, it’s about training yourself to understand exactly what's happening and act fast, protect, and guide everyone in your home. Without punishment. Without guilt. And without guessing. 👇 Here’s what we cover: — The truth about toddler sensory behavior & how it triggers your dog— Why growling is not a bad thing— What resource guarding actually looks like (and how to handle it)— The #1 mistake parents make with toddlers and dogs— My exact step-by-step training plan to restore calm and create safety Whether you’re already in the thick of it or trying to prep before baby arrives, you’ll walk away from this episode feeling clear, supported, and ready to lead. ✨ Book a 1:1 Dog + Baby Strategy Session:I’m now offering limited spots for 1:1 online private session to help you create a safer, more structured plan for life with your dog and baby. These are fully personalized and rooted in real-life, not one-size-fits-all advice.👉 Book your session here(Spots are limited...I only take a 4 per month!) 🐾 Free Gift: Dog + Baby Prep ChecklistWant a free checklist to help you set your dog up for success before baby even arrives?Message me the word “guide” on my latest post at @woofandwomb and I’ll DM it to you! 📖 Resource Mentioned: Dog Body Language BookIf you want to get better at reading your dog before things escalate, this is the book I always recommend:👉 Doggie Language by Lili Chin

title: A Dog's Life Archive: Dr Nick Thompson
podcast_name: A Dog's Life with Anna Webb
summary: Our archive wellness season continues with this classic episode where Anna is joined by Dr Nick Thompson, known as the Holistic Vet. We chat about vaccines, but also about minimising chemicals in your home. Science proves that indoor environments can be 100 times more toxic than outdoors. With this in mind Dr Nick talks about his experience using ‘probiotic therapy’. We chat about his experience with his own dogs in terms of Dental Care and with Dog Dental Month upcoming, it's a timely reminder that tooth brushing is not always the full proof answer in dogs or indeed humans. This why diet plays a major role. Dr Nick has long championed feeding a raw balanced and complete diet and we chat about why keeping your dog’s mouth full of good bacteria, rather than creating a sterile environment created by sterile diets, is so easy yet important!  Biscuit kibble gets stuck in dogs gums, and tiny particles remain overtime to create a pro-inflammatory reaction and gum disease. Conversely, feeding raw fuels the digestive system, the microbiome, and creates an environment in the mouth for good bacteria to flourish and counter any bad bacteria, which helps keep disease at bay. This coupled with regular treatment with Ingenious Probiotics’ Dental Spray has Dr Nick’s thumb’s up. Similarly for dogs fed on kibble diets especially adding some Collar-gen will replenish what is missing from a sterile biscuit diet. Collagen depletes in all mammals with age, and as a structural protein is essential for strong bones, and healthy soft tissue, including teeth and gums.  Nick's Website Nick's Instagram If you love A Dog's Life and would like to help support the show why not become a Patreon backer where you can also have access to some exclusive content.  If you want to move your dog to a raw diet or even switch brands we wholly recommend Paleo Ridge For more about Anna go to annawebb.co.uk Music and production by Mike Hanson for Pod People Productions

title: Dog Refusing Treats? How to Help Them Have Stress Free Walks
podcast_name: Stress Free Dog Walks
summary: Why Your Dog Refuses Treats on Walks (Even the Really Tasty Ones) Your dog loves treats at home - so why won’t they take them outside? If you’ve tried high-value treats, expensive snacks, or even homemade liver cake… and your dog still won’t eat, it’s not that they’re just ‘not interested’. It’s one of the most common (and misunderstood) challenges for dog owners, especially if your dog is pulling, barking, lunging, or totally ignoring you. In this episode, we’re digging into why dogs refuse food on walks (even when they’re not fussy at home) and what that tells you about their state of mind. You'll learn how stress, overstimulation, or anxiety affect appetite, and how that connects to dog behaviour problems on walks.  Plus you’ll get my tips for helping them eat - so you can train your dog effectively, whether it’s for recall, loose lead walking, calm greetings or anything in between! 🔔 Subscribe for weekly tips to help your dog stay calm, focused and well behaved!  "Having a dog who happily eats on walks is a huge advantage - it unlocks a world of training opportunities." 💬 📈Dog Walking Diagnostic - Discover Your Dog’s Walking Style!  Do you know the REAL reason your dog pulls, barks, lunges or ignores you? It’s often not what you think!  Discover why your dog struggles on walks—and more importantly, what you can do about it. The Dog Walking Diagnostic Quiz is completely free, takes just 5 mins, and could be your first step toward calmer, stress-free walks with a well-behaved dog you’re proud of. https://stressfreedogwalks.scoreapp.com/  🐕 Calm Canine Blueprint Leave a rating/review, and head over to https://stressfreedogwalks.com/review to upload your screenshot. Then I’ll send you my Calm Canine Blueprint completely free as a gift.  🍿 Watch the YouTube Show https://www.youtube.com/@StressFreeDogWalks  💬 Connect with me and join the conversation! title: Are R+ Trainers Frauds? Can A Dog Come When Called Around Wildlife Without Using Punishers? #305
podcast_name: Shaped by Dog with Susan Garrett
summary: Visit us at shapedbydog.com      Can you really stop a dog from chasing deer or anything else without an e-collar? Yes, and I’m sharing how. There’s an ongoing commentary that it's impossible to train certain dogs, especially those with high prey drive, to come when called using only reinforcement-based methods. Now, those of us who are reinforcement-based trainers know that it's absolutely possible. The secret lies in understanding the Canine Predatory Motor Sequence. Rather than trying to stop dogs at the end of the sequence when it's way too late, we focus on the early stages and what we want our dogs to do.      In this episode, you’ll hear:     • Why some say reinforcement-based training alone can’t stop high prey drive dogs.  • How the canine predatory motor sequence works and why it matters for your dog’s behavior  • Why suppressing behavior isn’t training, and what I do instead.  • The three common ways people deal with behavior problems and which one really works.  • How I use predatory redirection to interrupt chase drive.  • A real-life example of Prophet ignoring deer just 15 feet away.  • How we changed Tater Salad’s behavior using relationship-building games.  • Special opportunity to join my Home School the Dog program.  • Why head halters can act like emergency brakes when you need them.  • How I redirect Kim’s rescue dog Waffles from distractions to focus back to me and what you can take away from it.     Join Home School the Dog:  To join Home School the Dog at a special discount, write to us at wag@dogsthat.com with the subject line: Prey Drive    Learn To Play ItsYerChoice:  Learn how to play the ItsYerChoice (IYC) Game - https://recallers.com/iycsummit-join/    Hand Target and More Foundational Games  Get the Hand Target Game and more foundational training games in our free Connected Dog Season of Nuance Video Series running for a limited time - https://learn.doggyflix.com/     Resources:     1. title: The Dopamine Secret: How to Make Your Dog Choose You Over the World
podcast_name: Listen Dog Training: The Walk & Talk
summary: Ever wondered why your dog seems more interested in a leaf blowing down the street than your recall cue? Or why their ears perk up at the sound of the fridge but not your voice? It all comes down to one powerful brain chemical: dopamine. In this episode of The Walk & Talk, I’m diving deep into the science of dopamine and how you can use it to massively boost your training results, strengthen your bond, and finally become more reinforcing than literally everything else in your dog’s world. Whether you’re working on recall, loose lead walking, or just want to be your dog’s favourite thing again—this episode is packed with insight and practical tweaks you can start using today.   In this episode, you’ll learn: 💡What dopamine actually is—and how it affects your dog’s behaviour 💡Why anticipation is just as powerful as the reward itself 💡How to make your training more engaging and brain-boosting 💡Simple ways to create dopamine spikes using rewards, patterns and movement 💡How to use surprise, variety and play to make YOU the most exciting part of your dog’s day 💡Real-life examples of tweaks that create faster, more reliable results   💻 Want to dive deeper into transforming your dog’s behaviour with science-backed strategies that actually work? Check out my digital training products, freebies and online courses at www.listendogtraining.com You can use code PODCAST10 for 10% off all my digital dog training packages, guides & courses!   📱 Let’s connect! I’d love to hear how you’re using the dopamine trick in your own training:  Follow me on Instagram and TikTok → @listendogtraining 🎧 If this episode gave you an “aha!” moment, I’d be so grateful if you left a review 🌟 or shared the episode with a fellow dog lover—it helps the podcast grow and helps more people train with confidence, clarity, and joy!

title: E303- Why Genetics Matter More Than Training — Dr. Stephanie Cottee
podcast_name: No Bad Dogs Podcast
summary: Dr. Stephanie Cottee is an animal behaviour and welfare professional with nearly two decades of experience applying scientific principles to real-world scenarios. She holds a BSc in Animal Behaviour, along with an MSc and PhD in Animal Behaviour & Welfare. Her career has spanned various sectors, including animal agriculture, corporate animal welfare leadership, and nutritional innovation. She has worked extensively in the implementation of best practices to improve the welfare of both farm and companion animals. She now focuses on supporting animal health through nutrition, management, and evidence-based solutions. In addition, she is a dog trainer and co-owner of Black Kraken K9, a breeder of working-line German Shepherds. You’ve probably heard that working with your dogs one at a time is the way to go, but what does that actually look like in real life? title: "FROM SCRIPT TO SHELTER" with Actor Kate Zenna
podcast_name: Fur Real with Mark A Kyle
summary: We’re joined by the incredibly multi-talented Kate Zenna — actor, director, producer, writer... and above all, a devoted dog rescuer.  Kate has been a lifelong dog lover and proudly lives with a pack of pups she’s rescued over the years. Her tireless commitment to giving dogs a second chance has earned her a title I use with the utmost admiration: the crazy dog lady — and trust me, it’s the highest of compliments.  In this heartwarming and inspiring episode, Kate shares how she channels her many creative talents into finding loving homes for countless dogs. Her stories are too numerous for a single podcast, but we dig into some of her most memorable rescues and the passion that fuels her mission.  We also get an exciting sneak peek at a new animal-centered film project she’s developing — you’ll hear about it here before almost anyone else.  Tune in for a powerful reminder of how much of a difference one person can make in the lives of animals in need.  www.furrealpodcast.com    ig @thefurrealpodcast     ig @markakyle  fb @ The Fur Real Podcast    fb @ Mark A Kyle  tik tok The Fur Real Podcast  ig @katezenna  fb Kate Zenna  Speical thanks to J Jig Cicero @jjigcicero for our music intro and outro..you rock!!! title: Training Holidays & Spicy Dogs: Adam Daines Brings the Fun
podcast_name: Positively Dog Training - The Official Victoria Stilwell Podcast
summary: We’re keeping it light this summer with episodes every other week so you can stay inspired without missing the sunshine. In this episode of the Victoria Stilwell Positively Podcast, Victoria sits down with UK-based trainer, and VSPDT, Adam Daines. Adam shares the story of how a difficult experience with traditional training methods as a 14-year-old shaped his unwavering commitment to positive reinforcement. Now running his successful business, Avon Dog Services, Adam opens up about his work with working-line dogs who often “go self-employed” when their natural needs aren’t being met. From practical enrichment ideas like tracking and “search and retrieve” games to his pioneering Training Holidays, Adam is redefining what pet dog training can look like. Plus, the two discuss their behind-the-scenes experiences on the Dog Academy series—camera interruptions and all! Whether you're a dog guardian struggling with reactivity or a future trainer trying to find your path, this episode is packed with insight, humor, and heart. To learn more about Adam, visit Avon Dog Services.

title: From Eventing to Para Dressage: Rebecca Page's Inspiring Journey Through Injury and Resilience
podcast_name: Let's Talk Horse Business
summary: George interviews Rebecca Page, a freelance dressage rider with a compelling journey from eventing to para dressage. Rebecca shares her experiences in the equestrian industry, including the challenges of maintaining a sustainable business while navigating the complexities of horse welfare and competition. She discusses her personal struggles with injuries, the importance of loyal owners, and the financial realities of running a horse business. The conversation also touches on the need for better public education about equestrian practices and the importance of unity within the equestrian community. As Rebecca looks to the future, she expresses her desire to continue progressing in her dressage career, aiming for international competitions and further development of her horses.  KEY TAKEAWAYS    Transition from Eventing to Para Dressage: After a serious injury that resulted in shattered ankles, the individual transitioned from eventing to para dressage, focusing on competing and training horses in a new discipline.    Challenges of the Equestrian Industry: The equestrian business is fraught with financial instability due to factors like unpredictable horse behavior, injuries, and the high costs associated with horse care and competition, making it difficult to maintain a steady income.    Support System: A strong support system, including loyal owners and family, is crucial for success in the equestrian world. The individual emphasized the importance of having supportive owners who are invested in the journey rather than just results.    Welfare and Public Perception: There is a growing concern about how the equestrian community is perceived by the public, particularly regarding horse welfare. The need for better education and communication about the realities of horse care and competition is highlighted.    Future Goals: The individual aims to continue progressing in their equestrian career, with aspirations to compete at higher levels in para dressage, including potential participation in international competitions, while also managing the challenges of running a freelance business.    Socials  https://www.instagram.com/gbetson/  www.tiktok.com/@georgebetson  About the Host  George Betson is a seasoned business entrepreneur with over a decade of experience, driven by a passion for both strategic business growth and the equine world. Starting his journey as a business graduate, George worked alongside his late father to run the family business, learning firsthand the value of hard work, resilience, and the essentials of building a successful enterprise.

title: Ep. 9. How to Reduce Your Dog’s Barking Before Baby Arrives: Dog and Baby Prep Tips
podcast_name: Dog and Baby Podcast: Dog training tips for expecting parents
summary: Is your dog’s barking already driving you up the wall and baby isn’t even here yet? In this episode, I’m breaking down how to reduce barking before your baby arrives, why it matters for your postpartum sanity, and what you can start doing right now to set your dog (and yourself) up for success. Whether it’s barking at the door, whining for attention, or just non-stop noise we’re tackling it all with realistic, mom-friendly strategies you can implement today. What You’ll Learn: ✔️ Why barking becomes a huge issue postpartum ✔️ What’s actually triggering your dog’s barking ✔️ Simple, low-effort ways to reduce barking during pregnancy ✔️ What not to do (and why yelling “no” doesn’t work) ✔️ How to prep your dog for a peaceful postpartum environment 🔗 Free Resource Mentioned: Grab my FREE Dog + Baby Prep Checklist to help you start creating a calmer, safer, more structured home for your dog before your baby arrives. ➡️ Get it here! ⸻ 📲 Stay Connected: Let’s keep this conversation going! Follow me on Instagram for more dog & baby prep tips, behind-the-scenes support, and free resources: ➡️ @woofandwomb If this episode helped you, share it with a mom friend, leave a review, and don’t forget to hit follow so you never miss an episode. dog barking baby, dog and baby prep, how to stop dog barking, preparing dog for baby, dog behavior during pregnancy, new baby dog training, dog barking postpartum, pregnancy dog tips, dog training for new moms, baby and dog transition

title: Mark's Horsemanship Clinics: What does he actually do?
podcast_name: Mark Langley Horsemanship Solutions for Partnership
summary: Horseman Mark Langley is renowned for his Horsemanship Clinics which fill months in advance. What is is that he looks for in horses as he assesses them and their owners needs and how does he train? Listen as he talks about a recent intensive clinic.

title: 5-Step Recovery Plan to Calm Your Dog After Barking, Lunging and Reactivity
podcast_name: Stress Free Dog Walks
summary: Your Dog Just Had a Meltdown? Here's EXACTLY What to Do Next (So the Whole Walk Isn't Ruined) Barking, lunging, spinning, pulling… if your dog just reacted on a walk, take a breath. This episode is your go-to recovery plan for what to do immediately after a dog meltdown - whether your dog barked at a stranger, lunged at another dog, or totally lost it over a squirrel. You'll learn the 5 Rs of Recovery strategy, a calming routine that helps reactive dogs, anxious dogs, or excitable dogs, so you can get through the rest of the walk without more chaos. Grab the FREE 5 Rs phone screensaver to help you remember the steps: https://stressfreedogwalks.com/recovery-guide   🔔 Subscribe to the show for more practical support and stress-free dog walk strategies.  "The reaction already happened. You can’t always prevent it - but what you do next determines everything." 💬 📈Dog Walking Diagnostic - Discover Your Dog’s Walking Style!  Do you know the REAL reason your dog pulls, barks, lunges or ignores you? It’s often not what you think!  Discover why your dog struggles on walks—and more importantly, what you can do about it. The Dog Walking Diagnostic Quiz is completely free, takes just 5 mins, and could be your first step toward calmer, stress-free walks with a well-behaved dog you’re proud of. https://stressfreedogwalks.scoreapp.com/  🐕 Calm Canine Blueprint Leave a rating/review, and head over to https://stressfreedogwalks.com/review to upload your screenshot. title: The Lie That Shaped Dog Training For Decades: The Truth About Dominance in Dogs + Why It’s Misunderstood
podcast_name: Listen Dog Training: The Walk & Talk
summary: What if everything you were told about being your dog’s ‘alpha’ was built on a misinterpretation of stressed-out wolves in captivity? In this eye-opening episode of The Walk & Talk, I take you on a deep-dive through the shaky science behind dominance theory, the myth of the ‘alpha dog,’ and the long-lasting impact this outdated ideology still has on modern dog training. You'll learn:  🐾 The real story behind those infamous wolf studies  🐾 Why dominance-based training harms trust, learning, and safety  🐾 What leadership should look like in a healthy dog-human relationship  🐾 How to recognise stress-based behaviours and unmet needs in your dog  🐾 The empowering truth: your dog isn’t trying to rule you—they’re trying to connect with you If you’ve ever felt torn between harsh training advice and doing what feels kind and intuitive, this episode will give you the clarity and confidence to lead your dog with compassion and structure. Let’s rewrite the rules—and reclaim the joy. 💡  Want to dive deeper? Explore my digital dog training courses designed to give you the exact tools to build connection, teach essential skills, and support a truly happy life with your dog—without the confusion or conflict.  Browse all courses at www.listendogtraining.com ✨  Free training tips, tutorials & support ⬇️  →  Instagram: @listendogtraining  →  TikTok: @listendogtraining
